channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Furniture on busy pavement, 3rd June
item.description:
People from 81 south gipsy rd moved out yesterday and left this stuff outside on busy pathway, Also bins didn't get collected and foxes have opened rubbish bags.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): South Gipsy Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin always needs emptying, 3rd June
item.description:
Thanks for following up This had got better but this bin constantly fills up (dogwalkers). it's near a garden / historic site / Military memorial chuch in a public park. ... so really should be in a better condition. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Grand Depot Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven and broken paving slabs, 3rd June
item.description:
Hi this is a trip hazard and can cause accidents to any of our visitors especially elderly residents that live here. I have reported this a few times and one side gets repaired leaving out other sides of the paving. Please could these be checked and repaired. Lots of broken tiles near corners and uneven height and depth of paving's. Thank you
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blunts Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep pothole near car park entrance, 3rd June
item.description:
Pothole located where cars enter and exit the car park with

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Upland Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgorwn large tree's, 3rd June
item.description:
Hi there, I have been reporting overgrown tree issue for the last 4-5 years and have not gotten any response nor seen any action from the Council. These are so overgrown and branches are hanging so low that they have now started to touch people's head. Elderly people live here and therefore due to sight issues they can get hit by a hanging branch. I trust you will action ASAP. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Friars Mews, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kilburn Lane (Premier Corner towards Banister Road), 3rd June
item.description:
The pothole has deteriorated significantly and is now affecting the safe passage of buses and other large vehicles. As buses approach the speed table, the uneven road surface causes the front of the vehicle to drop sharply before climbing onto the speed table, creating a risk of contact between the road surface and the front bumper or underbody. In addition, the road surface after the speed table has become increasingly depressed due to the deepening pothole. As buses descend from the speed table, the rear of the vehicle comes extremely close to the surface of the speed table, creating a risk of the rear bumper or underbody striking the road. The issue is becoming more pronounced as the pothole continues to deepen. This defect poses a risk of vehicle damage and could potentially affect passenger safety and comfort. Given the size and weight of buses regularly using this route, the condition of the road is likely to worsen further if not addressed promptly. I respectfully request that the council arrange an urgent inspection and undertake the necessary repairs to restore the road surface and remove the hazard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of rubbish near 31 - 33 Amhurst Road estates in and around the grass areas., 3rd June
item.description:
Lots of rubbish near 31 - 33 Amhurst Road estates in and around the grass areas.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti Tagging on Lambeth Palace Road, 3rd June
item.description:
The words ABAR (believed to mean Anti Bias Anti Racist) are sprayed on the stone pillars that are between pavement and green area. It's on Lambeth Palace Road close to Lambeth Bridge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albert Embankment, Lambeth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Barriers near 133 Amhurst Road blocking pavement dumped sink on grass and carpets., 3rd June
item.description:
Barriers near 133 Amhurst Road blocking pavement dumped sink on grass and carpets.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside number 1-5 Torbay Road, 3rd June
item.description:
Dropped kerbstones have been poorly repaired. Problem not rectified and now causing large puddle. Access compromised and falls have been reported due to very poor uneven repair and continuing dropped kerbstones and puddle. Also a large tree stump has not been removed in this area causing further access issues. Very poor maintenance all round. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Torbay Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Branch failure near main road, 3rd June
item.description:
The branch of a large Cedar tree has fallen on the drive of Kano, The Park (DA14 6AL). This might be “branch drop” (long dry spell followed by rapid uptake of water after rain). Google suggests other branches might be similarly vulnerable. Most other branches are over the busy Elm Road. Kano is largely unoccupied as we are trying to sell it with my mother, the owner, in a care home. Kano does have visitors though, gardeners, estate agents, myself and sister. A branch overhangs the house. I have contacted a tree surgeon. He has visited recently and I provided details to the land charges dept. A tree inspector is due to visit. We believed thinning it might help somewhat but now we’re concerned about all the branches engendering traffic and pedestrians. Neal Zimmermann 07799330302

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Park, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fernhead Road (Premier Corner By Kilburn Lane), 3rd June
item.description:
The pothole has become a significant road safety concern, particularly for buses and other large vehicles. When buses approach the speed hump, the deep pothole causes the front of the bus to dip sharply, creating a risk of damage to the front bumper and undercarriage. As the bus passes over the speed hump and exits, there is also a risk that the rear bumper may strike the road surface due to the uneven road condition. This defect presents a danger to public transport vehicles, passengers, and other road users. Continued deterioration of the road surface could lead to vehicle damage, service disruptions, or accidents. I request that the council urgently inspect the location and carry out the necessary repairs to restore the road surface and eliminate the hazard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cornwallis Close, Slade Green, Greater London
